page.title: Maiora 26m: Best Boat in Marbella for Long-range sotogrande or gibraltar runs

data.meta_description: Maiora 26m: best for long-range runs to Sotogrande or Gibraltar from Marbella. 12 pax, 26m, Puerto Banús. Book your luxury charter today.
data.summary: Discover why the Maiora 26m is the best boat in Marbella for long-range excursions to Sotogrande or Gibraltar. With 26m of deck space and 12-pax capacity, this flybridge cruiser departs Puerto Banús fully crewed and stocked.
key_facts: Length: 26m (85 ft) – largest in our fleet for long runs
Ideal for day trips to Sotogrande (20 NM) or Gibraltar (40 NM)
Includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance & IVA
Pricing on request – WhatsApp for custom quotes
Flybridge layout with sun pads, dining area, and shaded saloon
Stabilised ride for open-sea comfort on longer passages

faq.q: How far can the Maiora 26m travel from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: With a 26m hull and efficient engines, the Maiora 26m comfortably covers 40+ NM each way. That means Sotogrande (20 NM, ~45 min) and Gibraltar (40 NM, ~1.5 h) are easy day trips. Overnight options to Estepona or Cabopino are also possible.

faq.q: Is the Maiora 26m suitable for open-sea conditions?
faq.a: Yes. The Maiora 26m is a semi-displacement motor yacht with stabilisers, offering a steady ride in moderate swell. For the Strait of Gibraltar or longer runs, it’s far more comfortable than smaller planing boats.

faq.q: What is the maximum passenger capacity on the Maiora 26m?
faq.a: The Maiora 26m is licensed for 12 passengers plus a professional skipper and two crew. This makes it ideal for group celebrations, corporate outings, or multi-family trips to Sotogrande or Gibraltar.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price for the Maiora 26m?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden extras. Pricing is customised per itinerary – request a quote via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on the Maiora 26m?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food and beverages. The galley has a fridge, freezer, and ice maker to keep everything chilled. We recommend pre-ordering catering for longer runs to Gibraltar or Sotogrande.

faq.q: What are the departure and return times for a Sotogrande trip?
faq.a: Typical departure from Puerto Banús is 10:00–11:00, arriving Sotogrande around 11:45–12:00. You can anchor off the Sotogrande marina for lunch, swim, or visit the beach club. Return by 18:00–19:00. Flexible on request.

faq.q: Does the Maiora 26m have a flybridge?
faq.a: Yes. The Maiora 26m features a large flybridge with sun loungers, a dining table, and a wet bar. It’s the best spot for panoramic views of the Costa del Sol coast, especially during sunset crossings back to Marbella.

faq.q: How do I book the Maiora 26m?
faq.a: Contact us directly via WhatsApp or the enquiry form on our website. We’ll confirm availability, tailor a quote for your preferred route (Sotogrande, Gibraltar, or Estepona), and arrange everything from crew to provisioning.

data.body_html:
For groups wanting to push beyond the Marbella coastline, the Maiora 26m is the best boat in Marbella for long-range runs to Sotogrande or Gibraltar. At 26 metres (85 ft) with a 12-passenger capacity, this flybridge motor yacht departs from Puerto Banús and combines ocean-going stability with five-star comfort. Whether you’re planning a day trip to Sotogrande’s marina or a full-day crossing to the Rock of Gibraltar, the Maiora 26m delivers range, space, and style.

Why the Maiora 26m is the best boat in Marbella for long-range runs

Most charter boats in Marbella are designed for short coastal hops – Cabopino, Cala del Faro, or a quick lap of the Golden Mile. The Maiora 26m is different. Its semi-displacement hull and stabilisers let it cruise comfortably at 18–22 knots in moderate seas, making it a genuine long-range option. A run from Puerto Banús to Sotogrande (20 NM) takes about 45 minutes; Gibraltar (40 NM) is 1.5 hours each way. That’s a realistic day trip with time to explore, swim, and dine ashore.

Specifications and layout

  • Length overall: 26 m (85 ft)
  • Beam: 6.2 m
  • Max passengers: 12 + 3 crew
  • Engines: Twin diesel (1,400+ HP combined)
  • Cruising speed: 18–22 kts
  • Departs: Puerto Banús, Marbella

The flybridge is the highlight: sun pads aft, a dining table forward, and a wet bar in between. Below, the saloon has a full galley, lounge seating, and a day head. On deck, the aft cockpit is shaded and leads to a large swim platform – ideal for cooling off between destinations.

Sotogrande: the ultimate lunch run

Sotogrande, 20 nautical miles west of Marbella, is a top destination for Maiora 26m charters. You can anchor off the Sotogrande marina for lunch at a beach club, or dock at the marina itself (subject to availability). The run takes you past Estepona’s coastline and the Sierra Bermeja foothills – a scenic 45-minute cruise. The Maiora’s stabilisers make the passage smooth, even when the afternoon Levante picks up. For a full day, combine Sotogrande with a swim stop at Cala Cortés or Cala del Faro on the return leg.

Gibraltar: a full-day adventure

Gibraltar is 40 NM from Puerto Banús – a 1.5-hour run at cruising speed. The Maiora 26m can make the round trip with plenty of time ashore. Once there, you can tie up at Gibraltar Marina, explore the Rock, visit the Barbary macaques, or simply enjoy lunch with a view of the Strait. The crossing itself passes the Bay of Algeciras and the Alboran Sea; keep an eye out for dolphins and, in summer, pilot whales. Because the Maiora 26m has a generator and full galley, you can also anchor off Catalan Bay for a private swim stop before heading back.

What’s included on every Maiora 26m charter

All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no add-on fees. For longer runs to Sotogrande or Gibraltar, we can arrange catering, extra crew, or a jet ski (ask about the Mangusta 80 if you want a free jet ski included). Pricing for the Maiora 26m is on request – contact us via WhatsApp for a tailored quote based on your itinerary and group size.

Comparing the Maiora 26m to other long-range boats

Our fleet has several large yachts, but the Maiora 26m stands out for long-range runs. The Ferretti 94 (29 m) is bigger and more luxurious, but its minimum charter is 4 hours and it’s priced accordingly. The Mangusta 80 (24 m) is sportier and includes a jet ski, but its open-plan layout is better for partying than cruising. The Maiora 26m strikes the balance: a proper flybridge cruiser that eats up miles without sacrificing comfort.

How to book the Maiora 26m

Booking is simple. Choose your date, preferred destination (Sotogrande, Gibraltar, or a custom route), and send us a message via WhatsApp or our enquiry form. We’ll confirm availability, send a quote, and handle everything from crew to provisioning. The Maiora 26m is based at Puerto Banús and can depart from 09:00. For a sunset return, aim for a 10:00–11:00 departure – that gives you time for a long lunch in Sotogrande or a full Gibraltar excursion.

Frequently asked questions

How far can the Maiora 26m travel from Puerto Banús?

With a 26m hull and efficient engines, the Maiora 26m comfortably covers 40+ NM each way. That means Sotogrande (20 NM, ~45 min) and Gibraltar (40 NM, ~1.5 h) are easy day trips. Overnight options to Estepona or Cabopino are also possible.

Is the Maiora 26m suitable for open-sea conditions?

Yes. The Maiora 26m is a semi-displacement motor yacht with stabilisers, offering a steady ride in moderate swell. For the Strait of Gibraltar or longer runs, it’s far more comfortable than smaller planing boats.

What is the maximum passenger capacity on the Maiora 26m?

The Maiora 26m is licensed for 12 passengers plus a professional skipper and two crew. This makes it ideal for group celebrations, corporate outings, or multi-family trips to Sotogrande or Gibraltar.

What is included in the charter price for the Maiora 26m?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden extras. Pricing is customised per itinerary – request a quote via WhatsApp.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on the Maiora 26m?

Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food and beverages. The galley has a fridge, freezer, and ice maker to keep everything chilled. We recommend pre-ordering catering for longer runs to Gibraltar or Sotogrande.

What are the departure and return times for a Sotogrande trip?

Typical departure from Puerto Banús is 10:00–11:00, arriving Sotogrande around 11:45–12:00. You can anchor off the Sotogrande marina for lunch, swim, or visit the beach club. Return by 18:00–19:00. Flexible on request.

Does the Maiora 26m have a flybridge?

Yes. The Maiora 26m features a large flybridge with sun loungers, a dining table, and a wet bar. It’s the best spot for panoramic views of the Costa del Sol coast, especially during sunset crossings back to Marbella.

How do I book the Maiora 26m?

Contact us directly via WhatsApp or the enquiry form on our website. We’ll confirm availability, tailor a quote for your preferred route (Sotogrande, Gibraltar, or Estepona), and arrange everything from crew to provisioning.
